Mobility-Data Epidemic Modeling and Surv: This domain examines how human mobility data, particularly from mobile phone call records, can be leveraged to model and track epidemic spread in developing countries, as seen in studies on COVID-19 in Nigeria during flooding and malaria elimination planning. It combines agent-based and spatiotemporal disease models with mobility signals, while also weighing the challenges of accessing such data and the pitfalls of big data analysis exemplified by the Google Flu parable. Additional readings address forecasting techniques like Bayesian structural time series and measuring how epidemic alerts alter movement patterns.
AI-Driven Vaccination and Immunization U: This domain focuses on using AI and optimization to increase vaccine uptake, centered on the ADVISER system deployed in Nigeria for child vaccination intervention. It also covers fair vaccination strategies via influence maximization, sequential vaccine allocation under delayed feedback, and Bayesian modeling to correct for maternal recall bias in immunization rate estimates in India.
Restless Bandits and Resource Allocation: This domain applies restless bandits and reinforcement learning to allocate scarce health resources fairly and effectively in underserved communities, including index-based treatment allocation and Bayesian online approaches. Readings address maternal and child healthcare in non-Markovian settings, deep reinforcement learning for equitable resource distribution, and planning multiple epidemic interventions.
AI-Assisted Medical Imaging Diagnosis in: This domain covers deep learning tools for diagnosing diseases from medical signals and images in low-resource areas, spanning cough-sound-based pulmonary screening, head and neck cancer triage, and pediatric oncology support for marginalized communities. Several readings focus specifically on detecting occupational lung diseases like silicosis and pneumoconiosis from chest X-rays.
Maternal, Child, and Reproductive Health: This domain explores digital tools and design approaches supporting maternal, child, and reproductive health in low-resource settings, including tele-triage for pregnant women in Kenya, self-directed learning for maternity workers, and OCR digitization of mother and child protection cards in India. It also addresses community cohesion among mothers in bandwidth-constrained settings, birth declaration to reduce child marriage in Bangladesh, tangible nutrition play in Peru, and causal analysis of contraceptive discontinuation.
Climate-Health and Air Pollution Sensing: This domain integrates climate and air-quality sensing with health outcomes, covering climate-driven epidemic prediction, foundation models for emission sources and respiratory infection hotspots, and transformer-based air pollution source mapping in South Asia. Readings also address mitigating kiln industry pollution, wildfire smoke plume segmentation from satellite imagery, high-resolution road-network pollutant quantification, and extreme weather impacts on physical activity and sleep.
Clean Cooking and Household Energy Trans: This domain addresses transitions to cleaner household cooking energy, using a health belief model and theory of change for service design in the slums of Kathmandu. It also assesses cooking energy behavior and the potential for shifting to electric cooking in informal urban settlements.
Human-Centered Global Health Device Desi: This domain focuses on designing and prototyping medical devices for global health, including life-saving warming technologies and stakeholder engagement through front-end device design. Readings also cover a usability study of an optical schistosomiasis diagnostic device in Nigeria and efforts to transform rural healthcare through technology crafts for the digitally underserved.
Designing for Stigmatized and Marginaliz: This domain examines human-centered design for stigmatized and marginalized populations, including Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh, caregivers of children with autism facing stigmatization, and adolescents managing Type 1 diabetes in LMICs. It also considers the paradoxes of India's vast population and untapped human resources within health care.
Secure and Agile mHealth Deployment in t: This domain addresses the operational, security, and organizational challenges of deploying mobile health systems in the field, including data classification for secure health data collection and security leadership where there is no CISO. It also covers applying agile principles to complex public health problems like malnutrition management and the adoption of mHealth services by internally displaced persons in Nigeria.
Health Information System (HIS/DHIS2) Im: This domain examines the deployment of open-source health information systems, particularly DHIS2, across low- and middle-income countries including Lao PDR, Mozambique, Lesotho, and Malawi. The readings explore how these systems can strengthen representation and inclusion of marginalized groups, while also documenting the complexity, distress, and social exclusion risks that arise during implementation. Technical themes include integrating community and facility health data through methods like machine learning and probabilistic record linkage.
mHealth Behavior Change and Frontline He: This domain covers the design and sustainability of mobile health interventions aimed at behavior change communication and supporting frontline health workers, with case studies from rural India and sub-Saharan Africa. It addresses both the strategic development of mHealth programs and the training and support practices needed to sustain them. It also includes technical approaches such as metric learning for handwritten digit recognition to expand the reach of voice-based communication platforms.
AI/ML for Clinical Prediction and Health: This domain applies artificial intelligence and machine learning to clinical prediction tasks such as diabetes forecasting, chronic disease management through lab test response prediction, and suicide risk detection, alongside foundational methods like contrastive learning and self-supervised time-series clustering. A strong equity thread runs throughout, with readings on quantifying health inequalities induced by data and AI, achieving gender fairness in mental health prediction, and mapping vulnerability among disadvantaged urban populations. It also spans related predictive applications including insurance fraud detection and satellite-imagery-based poverty estimation.
Digital Technology in Pandemic Response: This domain focuses on the social and technological dimensions of the COVID-19 pandemic in South Asia, examining impacts on poor and vulnerable groups in India and gender inequality among healthcare workers in Pakistan. It highlights the role of technology in enabling risk communication and community engagement, drawing on experiences from Bangladesh during the pandemic.
